---
title: "Quarto Documents"
---

`ir run` renders `.qmd` and `.Rmd` files with `quarto render`. Dependencies live under an `ir:` key in the document YAML.

```yaml
---
title: "My report"
ir:
  dependencies:
    - dplyr>=1.0
    - gt@1.0
  r-version: ">= 4.0"
  exclude-newer: "2024-01-15"
---
```

Run the document with the same command used for scripts:

```console
$ ir run report.qmd
$ ir run report.qmd --to pdf
$ ir run --vanilla report.qmd
```

`ir` resolves the document dependencies into the same cached library model used for scripts, then launches `quarto render`. Leading Rscript options are forwarded to Quarto's knitr engine. Trailing arguments are passed to `quarto render`.

When an R version is selected by frontmatter or `--r-version`, `ir` pins Quarto's knitr R to that selection.

Set `IR_QUARTO` to use a specific Quarto executable. Rscript options containing commas cannot be forwarded, because Quarto reads `QUARTO_KNITR_RSCRIPT_ARGS` as a comma-separated value with no escaping.

## rmarkdown injection

A Quarto render needs the `rmarkdown` package for the knitr engine. `ir` appends `rmarkdown` to the resolved dependencies automatically when it is not already present, pulling the latest version (or the latest as of `exclude-newer` when set).

Declare `rmarkdown` yourself under `ir.dependencies` — directly (e.g. `rmarkdown@2.29`) or transitively, such as through `quarto` — to override the injected seed.
